headline: Echoblast — Wikipedia article as of 2027-12-31
rendered_text:
# Echoblast

*From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia*

**Echoblast, Inc.** is an American [cloud computing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cloud_computing) company specializing in [GPU](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphics_processing_unit) rental and managed artificial intelligence workloads.[1] Headquartered in [San Francisco, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco), Echoblast operates a hybrid infrastructure model combining owned datacenter capacity with a third-party GPU marketplace.[2] The company was founded in January 2025 and is a member of [Y Combinator](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Y_Combinator)'s Winter 2025 cohort.[3] As of late 2027, Echoblast serves individual developers, research teams, and enterprise customers seeking on-demand GPU compute and managed AI training and inference services.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Private (Delaware C-corporation) |
| **Industry** | Cloud computing, Artificial intelligence infrastructure |
| **Founded** | January 2025 |
| **Founders** | Will Coleman, Mark Howell, Angela Holmes, Mark Leonard |
| **Headquarters** | 500 Sansome Street, San Francisco, CA 94111 |
| **Key people** | Will Coleman (CEO), Mark Howell (CTO), Angela Holmes (President), Mark Leonard (Chief Architect) |
| **Products** | Echoblast Cloud, Echoblast Train, Echoblast Inference |
| **Website** | echoblast.ai |

## History

### Founding and Y Combinator (2025)

Echoblast was incorporated as a Delaware C-corporation in January 2025.[4] The company was founded by four co-founders: Will Coleman (CEO), Mark Howell (Chief Technology Officer), Angela Holmes (President), and Mark Leonard (Chief Architect).[5] The founding team was accepted into [Y Combinator](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Y_Combinator)'s Winter 2025 batch, a cohort focused on infrastructure and AI-systems companies.[3] The company's domain, echoblast.ai, was registered during the early founding period and serves as the primary web presence for customer acquisition and product access.

### Early growth (2025–2027)

Following Y Combinator acceptance, Echoblast began development of its core GPU rental platform and marketplace infrastructure. The company's initial go-to-market focused on individual machine learning researchers and small teams seeking affordable, on-demand GPU compute without long-term contracts.[6] By late 2025, Echoblast had launched its self-service Cloud product, allowing developers to provision and manage GPU instances through a web console and API.

In 2026, the company expanded its product portfolio to include Echoblast Train, a managed training platform that abstracts away infrastructure management for large-scale model training workloads. The Train product bundled GPU compute with orchestration, distributed training libraries, and checkpoint management, targeting teams training large language models and computer vision systems.[7]

By 2027, Echoblast had established a hybrid operational model: a combination of owned GPU datacenter capacity and a third-party marketplace connecting customers to external datacenter providers. This marketplace approach allowed the company to scale supply without proportional capital expenditure while maintaining a premium owned-fleet tier for latency-sensitive and reserved-capacity customers.[2]

## Products

### Echoblast Cloud

Echoblast Cloud is the company's core self-service GPU rental offering. It provides on-demand access to GPU instances (including [NVIDIA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nvidia) A100, H100, and other accelerators) via a web dashboard and programmatic API.[8] Customers are charged hourly for compute usage, with no long-term commitment required. The Cloud product integrates with the third-party marketplace, allowing customers to select between owned Echoblast capacity and external provider capacity based on price and availability.

### Echoblast Train

Echoblast Train is a managed machine learning training platform launched in 2026. It automates distributed training orchestration, gradient checkpointing, and fault tolerance for large model training runs. Train customers specify training job parameters (model, dataset, hyperparameters) and the platform handles GPU allocation, data loading, and job monitoring. The product targets research teams and companies training foundation models.[7]

### Echoblast Inference

Echoblast Inference, launched in late 2026, provides managed inference serving for deployed models. It handles auto-scaling, batching, and multi-model serving on GPU clusters, abstracting infrastructure complexity from application developers and ML engineers.

## Corporate affairs

### Leadership

As of late 2027, Echoblast's executive team consists of four co-founders in senior leadership roles: Will Coleman serves as Chief Executive Officer, Mark Howell as Chief Technology Officer, Angela Holmes as President, and Mark Leonard as Chief Architect.[5] The company has assembled a broader leadership team including VP of Sales, VP of Customer Success, and VP of Operations, though specific names and appointment dates remain undisclosed in public filings.

### Board of directors

Echoblast's board of directors includes the four co-founders and investor representatives from Y Combinator and early-stage venture capital firms. Specific board composition as of 2027 has not been widely disclosed.

## Data center footprint

As of late 2027, Echoblast operates or has access to GPU capacity in multiple regions:

- **San Francisco, California**: Company headquarters and primary engineering office at 500 Sansome Street.
- **Owned datacenter capacity**: Echoblast operates proprietary GPU clusters in at least one U.S. region, though specific datacenter locations and provider partnerships have not been publicly disclosed in detail.
- **Third-party marketplace**: The company's marketplace aggregates GPU capacity from external datacenter providers, allowing customers to access geographically distributed infrastructure.
